register.note: The lottery histogram is a full-content-width, panel-framed, mono-captioned protagonist that reads as 'spread vs one stable line' even at thumbnail size; pin labels now de-collided onto separate rows.

The hero shows the lesson's mechanism — the distribution of test error over 80 splits with train pinned on the optimistic flank and CV dropping a stable estimate — as an outcome distribution rather than the residual-square construction Seeing-Theory draws.

The See-it graphic self-describes (kicker, labelled train bar, axis cues, train-vs-test readout with 'seen — flatters'/'honest') and prose tints matching key terms, at parity with Distill's captioned small-multiples.
Warm off-white ground, hairline rules, restrained mono micro-labels, AA viz-ink terms and clear focal hierarchy give a Distill-soft finish; the pin-label collision is resolved.
One explanatory hero load motion (histogram fills then CV pin drops, static under reduced-motion) plus learner-driven reshuffle/slider re-renders; explanatory not decorative like 3B1B's stepped cost.

The prior seam is fixed — CV reads accent-teal on both the strip pin and the readout, bars stay viz-prediction blue, error language reserved, prose pairs hue with weight+underline; coherent lab-wide grammar (was 2).

hero.note: Lottery histogram with train/CV pins; reference-pin labels now de-collided so 'train — flatters' and 'CV — stable' never overprint.

assessment.note: Explain-it foregrounds an open/interactive transfer prompt above the recall items with feedback on every option.

verdict.summary: Hero added and colour seam fixed — every register dimension clears floor 3.
